packages.name: lodash
packages.synopsis: Package lodash is a comprehensive, type-safe functional utility library for Go, inspired by the JavaScript library lodash.
packages.doc:
Package lodash is a comprehensive, type-safe functional utility library for
Go, inspired by the JavaScript library lodash. It is implemented entirely
with the Go standard library and leans on Go 1.24 generics so that helpers
stay type-safe without external dependencies.

All functions are pure and non-mutating unless explicitly documented
otherwise: they return new slices or maps rather than modifying their
arguments. Any randomness is injected via a *math/rand.Rand so that behavior
is fully deterministic and testable.

# Collections and slices

Transform, query and reshape slices:

	Map, MapI, Filter, Reject, Reduce, ReduceRight, ForEach, ForEachI,
	ForEachRight, Find, FindLast, FindIndex, FindLastIndex, Every, Some, None,
	Includes, IndexOf, LastIndexOf, IndexOfFrom, LastIndexOfFrom, GroupBy,
	KeyBy, CountBy, Partition, Uniq, UniqBy, SortedUniq, SortedUniqBy, Chunk,
	Flatten, FlattenDeep, FlattenDepth, FlatMap, Compact, Reverse, Zip, ZipWith,
	Unzip, UnzipWith, ZipObject, Difference, DifferenceBy, DifferenceWith,
	Intersection, IntersectionBy, IntersectionWith, Union, UnionBy, UnionWith,
	Xor, XorBy, XorWith, Without, Pull, PullAll, PullAllBy, PullAt, Remove,
	Take, TakeRight, TakeWhile, TakeRightWhile, Drop, DropRight, DropWhile,
	DropRightWhile, Head, First, Last, Nth, Initial, Tail, Slice, Join, Sample,
	SampleN, Shuffle, SortBy, OrderBy, SortedIndex, SortedIndexBy, SortedIndexOf,
	SortedLastIndex, SortedLastIndexBy, SortedLastIndexOf, Concat, Fill.

# Math and numbers

Aggregate, constrain and compute over numeric data:

	Sum, SumBy, Mean, MeanBy, Min, MinBy, Max, MaxBy, Add, Subtract, Multiply,
	Divide, Clamp, Round, Floor, Ceil, Random, RandomFloat, Range, RangeStep,
	RangeRight, InRange.

# Objects and maps

Inspect and reshape maps, including nested map[string]any access with dotted
and bracket paths:

	Keys, SortedKeys, Values, Entries, FromEntries, ToPairs, FromPairs,
	ZipObject, Pick, PickBy, Omit, OmitBy, MapKeys, MapValues, Invert, InvertBy,
	Merge, MergeWith, Assign, AssignWith, Defaults, DefaultsDeep, Transform,
	ForOwn, FindKey, Size, Get, GetOr, Has, At, Result, Set, Unset, Update,
	ToPath.

# Strings

Case conversion, formatting, escaping and templating:

	Words, CamelCase, PascalCase, SnakeCase, KebabCase, StartCase, LowerCase,
	UpperCase, ToLower, ToUpper, Capitalize, UpperFirst, LowerFirst, Pad,
	PadStart, PadEnd, Truncate, Repeat, Deburr, Trim, TrimStart, TrimEnd,
	Escape, Unescape, EscapeRegExp, Replace, Split, StartsWith, EndsWith,
	Template.

# Lang and value helpers

Clone, compare and interrogate arbitrary values:

	Clone, CloneDeep, IsEqual, Eq, IsEmpty, IsMatch, IsNil, IsPlainObject,
	IsString, IsBool, IsNumber, IsInteger, IsSlice, IsMap, IsError,
	IsObjectLike, IsNaN, IsFinite, CastArray, ToArray, DefaultTo, Gt, Gte, Lt,
	Lte, ToNumber, ToInteger, ToFinite, ToSafeInteger, ToString, Conforms,
	ConformsTo.

# Function combinators

Compose, adapt and constrain functions:

	Curry, Curry3, Partial, PartialRight, Flow, FlowRight, Compose, Cond, Over,
	OverEvery, OverSome, OverArgs, Negate, Ary, Unary, Flip, Rearg, Spread,
	NthArg, Wrap, Once, Memoize, MemoizeBy, NewDebouncer, NewThrottler, After,
	Before.

# Utilities and sequences

General-purpose helpers and a lazy value wrapper for method chaining:

	Identity, Constant, Noop, Times, UniqueID, Property, PropertyOf, Matches,
	Attempt, StubArray, StubObject, StubString, StubTrue, StubFalse, Chain
	(with Value, Thru and Tap).

types.name: CondPair
types.doc:
CondPair couples a predicate with the transform to run when it matches, for
use with Cond.

types.name: Debouncer
types.doc:
Debouncer wraps a function so that it only runs after calls have stopped for a
specified quiet period. Each call to Call resets the timer; the wrapped
function runs once, wait after the final call. Use Cancel to abort a pending
invocation. Debouncer is safe for concurrent use and schedules the call on a
separate goroutine via a time.Timer.

funcs.name: NewDebouncer
funcs.signature: func NewDebouncer(wait time.Duration, fn func()) *Debouncer
funcs.doc:
NewDebouncer creates a Debouncer that will invoke fn once calls have been
quiet for wait.

methods.name: Call
methods.doc:
Call schedules the wrapped function to run after the quiet period, resetting
any previously scheduled invocation.

methods.name: Cancel
methods.doc:
Cancel stops any pending invocation. It reports whether a pending call was
cancelled.

types.name: Entry
types.doc: Entry is a single key/value pair from a map.

funcs.name: Entries
funcs.signature: func Entries[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V) []Entry[K, V]
funcs.doc: Entries returns the key/value pairs of the map in unspecified order.

types.name: Number
types.doc: Number is a constraint permitting any built-in numeric type.

types.name: Order
types.doc: Order describes a sort direction for OrderBy.

types.name: Ordered
types.doc:
Ordered constrains the types that support the relational operators used by
Gt, Gte, Lt and Lte.

types.name: Pair
types.doc: Pair holds two related values produced by Zip.

funcs.name: ToPairs
funcs.signature: func ToPairs[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V) []Pair[K, V]
funcs.doc:
ToPairs returns the key/value pairs of the map as a slice of Pair values in
unspecified order.

funcs.name: Zip
funcs.signature: func Zip[A, B any](a []A, b []B) []Pair[A, B]
funcs.doc:
Zip combines two slices into a slice of pairs. The result length equals the
length of the shorter input.

types.name: Seq
types.doc:
Seq is a lightweight value wrapper that enables lodash-style method chaining.
A Seq carries a single value through a pipeline of transforms; call Value to
unwrap the final result. Because Go methods cannot introduce new type
parameters, transforms that change the value's type are provided as the
package-level functions Thru and Tap.

funcs.name: Chain
funcs.signature: func Chain[T any](value T) Seq[T]
funcs.doc: Chain wraps value in a Seq so that transforms can be applied fluently.

funcs.name: Tap
funcs.signature: func Tap[T any](s Seq[T], fn func(T)) Seq[T]
funcs.doc:
Tap invokes fn with the value wrapped by s for its side effects and returns
the Seq unchanged.

funcs.name: Thru
funcs.signature: func Thru[T, R any](s Seq[T], fn func(T) R) Seq[R]
funcs.doc:
Thru applies fn to the value wrapped by s and returns a new Seq holding the
(possibly differently typed) result.

methods.name: Tap
methods.doc:
Tap invokes fn with the wrapped value for its side effects and returns the
Seq unchanged, allowing you to observe an intermediate value mid-chain.

methods.name: Thru
methods.signature: func (s Seq[T]) Thru(fn func(T) T) Seq[T]
methods.doc:
Thru applies a same-typed transform to the wrapped value and returns a new
Seq holding the result. Use the package-level Thru function to transform the
value into a different type.

methods.name: Value
methods.doc: Value returns the wrapped value, terminating the chain.

types.name: SetSeq
types.doc:
SetSeq is a fluent wrapper around a slice of comparable elements. It
complements SliceSeq with the set-oriented operations that require the
element type to be comparable (deduplication, membership and the algebraic
set combinators). Call Value to unwrap the final slice.

funcs.name: ChainSet
funcs.signature: func ChainSet[T comparable](s []T) SetSeq[T]
funcs.doc:
ChainSet wraps s in a SetSeq so that set-oriented transforms can be applied
fluently.

methods.name: Compact
methods.doc: Compact removes zero-valued elements (for example 0, "" or false).

methods.name: Difference
methods.doc: Difference removes the elements that appear in any of others.

methods.name: Filter
methods.signature: func (s SetSeq[T]) Filter(pred func(T) bool) SetSeq[T]
methods.doc: Filter keeps only the elements for which pred returns true.

methods.name: Includes
methods.doc:
Includes reports whether value is present in the wrapped slice. It terminates
the chain.

methods.name: IndexOf
methods.doc:
IndexOf returns the index of the first occurrence of value, or -1 when it is
absent. It terminates the chain.

methods.name: Intersection
methods.doc: Intersection keeps only the elements that also appear in every one of others.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc: Reverse reverses the order of the wrapped elements.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the number of wrapped elements.

methods.name: Union
methods.doc:
Union returns the unique union of the wrapped slice with others, preserving
first-seen order.

methods.name: Uniq
methods.doc:
Uniq removes duplicate elements, keeping the first occurrence of each in
order.

methods.name: Value
methods.doc: Value returns the wrapped slice, terminating the chain.

methods.name: Without
methods.doc: Without removes every element equal to any of values.

types.name: SliceSeq
types.doc:
SliceSeq is a fluent wrapper around a slice that enables lodash-style method
chaining for transforms that preserve the element type. Because Go methods
cannot introduce new type parameters, element-type-changing operations (such
as mapping []T to []R) remain package-level functions; SliceSeq covers the
large family of slice operations that keep the same element type. Call Value
to unwrap the final slice.

funcs.name: ChainSlice
funcs.signature: func ChainSlice[T any](s []T) SliceSeq[T]
funcs.doc:
ChainSlice wraps s in a SliceSeq so that same-type slice transforms can be
applied fluently.

methods.name: Chunk
methods.doc:
Chunk splits the wrapped slice into groups of at most size elements. It
returns the groups directly, terminating the chain, because Go's type system
disallows a method returning a SliceSeq re-parameterised over []T.

methods.name: Concat
methods.doc: Concat appends the elements of others after the wrapped elements.

methods.name: Drop
methods.doc: Drop removes the first n elements.

methods.name: DropRight
methods.doc: DropRight removes the last n elements.

methods.name: DropWhile
methods.signature: func (s SliceSeq[T]) DropWhile(pred func(T) bool) SliceSeq[T]
methods.doc: DropWhile removes leading elements while pred returns true.

methods.name: Filter
methods.signature: func (s SliceSeq[T]) Filter(pred func(T) bool) SliceSeq[T]
methods.doc: Filter keeps only the elements for which pred returns true.

methods.name: ForEach
methods.doc:
ForEach invokes fn for each element and returns the SliceSeq unchanged,
allowing side effects mid-chain.

methods.name: Head
methods.doc:
Head returns the first element and true, or the zero value and false when the
slice is empty. It terminates the chain.

methods.name: Initial
methods.doc: Initial removes the last element.

methods.name: IsEmpty
methods.doc: IsEmpty reports whether the wrapped slice has no elements.

methods.name: Join
methods.doc:
Join concatenates the string forms of the wrapped elements separated by sep.
It terminates the chain.

methods.name: Last
methods.doc:
Last returns the final element and true, or the zero value and false when the
slice is empty. It terminates the chain.

methods.name: Nth
methods.doc:
Nth returns the element at index n (negative counts from the end) and true,
or the zero value and false when out of range. It terminates the chain.

methods.name: Reject
methods.signature: func (s SliceSeq[T]) Reject(pred func(T) bool) SliceSeq[T]
methods.doc: Reject removes the elements for which pred returns true.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.doc: Reverse reverses the order of the wrapped elements.

methods.name: Sample
methods.doc:
Sample returns a random element and true, or the zero value and false when
the slice is empty, drawing from rng. It terminates the chain.

methods.name: Shuffle
methods.doc:
Shuffle returns a randomly reordered copy of the wrapped slice using rng, so
results are deterministic when rng is seeded.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the number of wrapped elements.

methods.name: Slice
methods.signature: func (s SliceSeq[T]) Slice(start, end int) SliceSeq[T]
methods.doc: Slice keeps the half-open index range [start, end).

methods.name: Tail
methods.doc: Tail removes the first element.

methods.name: Take
methods.doc: Take keeps the first n elements.

methods.name: TakeRight
methods.doc: TakeRight keeps the last n elements.

methods.name: TakeWhile
methods.signature: func (s SliceSeq[T]) TakeWhile(pred func(T) bool) SliceSeq[T]
methods.doc: TakeWhile keeps leading elements while pred returns true.

methods.name: Tap
methods.doc:
Tap invokes fn with the wrapped slice for its side effects and returns the
SliceSeq unchanged.

methods.name: Value
methods.doc: Value returns the wrapped slice, terminating the chain.

types.name: TemplateOptions
types.doc:
TemplateOptions configures the delimiters recognized by Template. Zero-value
fields fall back to the lodash defaults: "<%=" ... "%>" for interpolation,
"<%-" ... "%>" for HTML-escaped interpolation and "<%" ... "%>" for evaluate
blocks.

types.name: Throttler
types.doc:
Throttler wraps a function so that it runs at most once per interval. The
first call runs immediately; subsequent calls within the interval are
suppressed. Throttler is safe for concurrent use.

funcs.name: NewThrottler
funcs.signature: func NewThrottler(interval time.Duration, fn func()) *Throttler
funcs.doc:
NewThrottler creates a Throttler that permits fn to run at most once per
interval.

methods.name: Call
methods.doc:
Call runs the wrapped function if at least interval has elapsed since the last
successful run (or if this is the first call). It reports whether the function
was invoked.

funcs.name: Add
funcs.signature: func Add[T Number](a, b T) T
funcs.doc: Add returns the sum of a and b.

funcs.name: After
funcs.signature: func After[R any](n int, fn func() R) func() (R, bool)
funcs.doc:
After returns a function that only invokes fn beginning with the nth call. The
first n-1 calls do nothing. It is safe for concurrent use.

funcs.name: Ary
funcs.signature: func Ary[T, R any](fn func(...T) R, n int) func(...T) R
funcs.doc:
Ary caps a variadic function so that it receives at most n arguments; any
extra arguments are dropped. A negative n is treated as zero.

funcs.name: Assign
funcs.signature: func Assign[K comparable, V any](dst map[K]V, sources ...map[K]V) map[K]V
funcs.doc:
Assign copies all key/value pairs from sources into dst, overwriting existing
keys, and returns dst.

funcs.name: AssignWith
funcs.signature: func AssignWith[K comparable, V any](dst map[K]V, fn func(dstV, srcV V) V, sources ...map[K]V) map[K]V
funcs.doc:
AssignWith copies key/value pairs from sources into a new map seeded with dst.
When a key already has a value, the resolver fn decides the result from the
destination value and the incoming source value. The input maps are not
mutated.

funcs.name: At
funcs.signature: func At(m map[string]any, paths ...string) []any
funcs.doc:
At returns the values found at each of the given paths, in order. Paths that
do not resolve yield a nil entry.

funcs.name: Attempt
funcs.signature: func Attempt[R any](fn func() R) (result R, err error)
funcs.doc:
Attempt invokes fn and recovers any panic, returning the panic value as an
error. On success it returns the function's result and a nil error.

funcs.name: Before
funcs.signature: func Before[R any](n int, fn func() R) func() R
funcs.doc:
Before returns a function that invokes fn only for the first n-1 calls. From
the nth call onward it returns the result of the last successful invocation.
It is safe for concurrent use.

funcs.name: CamelCase
funcs.signature: func CamelCase(s string) string
funcs.doc: CamelCase converts a string to camelCase.

funcs.name: Capitalize
funcs.signature: func Capitalize(s string) string
funcs.doc:
Capitalize upper-cases the first character of the string and lower-cases the
rest.

funcs.name: CastArray
funcs.signature: func CastArray[T any](values ...T) []T
funcs.doc:
CastArray wraps the provided values into a slice. Called with a single value
it yields a one-element slice; called with none it yields an empty slice.

funcs.name: Ceil
funcs.signature: func Ceil(value float64, precision int) float64
funcs.doc:
Ceil rounds value up to precision decimal places. A negative precision rounds
to the left of the decimal point. Like lodash's _.ceil it shifts the decimal
exponent to avoid floating-point error, so Ceil(4.016, 2) is exactly 4.02.

funcs.name: Chunk
funcs.signature: func Chunk[T any](s []T, size int) [][]T
funcs.doc:
Chunk splits the slice into groups of at most size elements. It panics if
size is less than 1.

funcs.name: Clamp
funcs.signature: func Clamp[T cmp.Ordered](value, lower, upper T) T
funcs.doc:
Clamp constrains value to lie within the inclusive range [lower, upper]. If
lower is greater than upper the bounds are swapped.

funcs.name: Clone
funcs.signature: func Clone[T any](value T) T
funcs.doc:
Clone returns a shallow copy of value. For maps and slices a new top-level
container is allocated whose elements are copied by assignment; nested
containers are shared with the original. For all other types the value is
returned unchanged (Go value semantics already copy it).

funcs.name: CloneDeep
funcs.signature: func CloneDeep[T any](value T) T
funcs.doc:
CloneDeep returns a deep copy of value. Maps, slices, arrays, pointers and
structs are recursively cloned so the result shares no mutable state with the
original. Channels, functions and unsafe pointers are copied by reference.

funcs.name: Compact
funcs.signature: func Compact[T comparable](s []T) []T
funcs.doc: Compact returns a new slice with all zero values removed.

funcs.name: Compose
funcs.signature: func Compose[T any](fns ...func(T) T) func(T) T
funcs.doc:
Compose composes the given same-typed transforms right to left. It is an alias
for FlowRight, matching the common functional-programming name.

funcs.name: Concat
funcs.signature: func Concat[T any](slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc: Concat returns a new slice with all provided slices concatenated together.

funcs.name: Cond
funcs.signature: func Cond[T, R any](pairs ...CondPair[T, R]) func(T) (R, bool)
funcs.doc:
Cond returns a function that runs the input through the first CondPair whose
predicate matches, returning that pair's transformed result. If none match it
returns the zero value of R and false.

funcs.name: Conforms
funcs.signature: func Conforms(spec map[string]func(any) bool) func(map[string]any) bool
funcs.doc:
Conforms builds a predicate that reports whether a map[string]any satisfies
every predicate in spec. Each spec entry names a key that must be present and
whose value must satisfy the associated predicate.

funcs.name: ConformsTo
funcs.signature: func ConformsTo(object map[string]any, spec map[string]func(any) bool) bool
funcs.doc: ConformsTo reports whether object satisfies every predicate in spec.

funcs.name: Constant
funcs.signature: func Constant[T any](value T) func() T
funcs.doc:
Constant returns a function that always returns value, ignoring any later
changes to the surrounding state.

funcs.name: CountBy
funcs.signature: func CountBy[T any, K comparable](s []T, fn func(T) K) map[K]int
funcs.doc: CountBy counts elements by the key returned by fn.

funcs.name: Curry
funcs.signature: func Curry[A, B, R any](fn func(A, B) R) func(A) func(B) R
funcs.doc:
Curry converts a two-argument function into a sequence of unary functions, so
that fn(a, b) can be called as Curry(fn)(a)(b).

funcs.name: Curry3
funcs.signature: func Curry3[A, B, C, R any](fn func(A, B, C) R) func(A) func(B) func(C) R
funcs.doc:
Curry3 converts a three-argument function into a sequence of unary functions,
so that fn(a, b, c) can be called as Curry3(fn)(a)(b)(c).

funcs.name: Curry4
funcs.signature: func Curry4[A, B, C, D, R any](fn func(A, B, C, D) R) func(A) func(B) func(C) func(D) R
funcs.doc:
Curry4 transforms a four-argument function into a sequence of unary functions
that supply the arguments from left to right. It extends the Curry/Curry3
family to arity four.

funcs.name: CurryRight
funcs.signature: func CurryRight[A, B, R any](fn func(A, B) R) func(B) func(A) R
funcs.doc:
CurryRight transforms a two-argument function into a sequence of unary
functions that supply the arguments from right to left: the returned function
takes the last argument first. It is the right-to-left analogue of Curry.

funcs.name: CurryRight3
funcs.signature: func CurryRight3[A, B, C, R any](fn func(A, B, C) R) func(C) func(B) func(A) R
funcs.doc:
CurryRight3 transforms a three-argument function into a sequence of unary
functions that supply the arguments from right to left. It is the
right-to-left analogue of Curry3.

funcs.name: Deburr
funcs.signature: func Deburr(s string) string
funcs.doc:
Deburr converts Latin-1 Supplement and Latin Extended-A letters in s to their
basic Latin equivalents and removes combining diacritical marks, matching
lodash's _.deburr. Ligatures expand to multiple characters (e.g. 'Æ' -> "Ae")
and Latin mathematical operators such as '×' and '÷' are left unchanged.

funcs.name: DefaultTo
funcs.signature: func DefaultTo[T comparable](value, fallback T) T
funcs.doc:
DefaultTo returns value unless it is the zero value for its type, in which
case it returns fallback.

funcs.name: Defaults
funcs.signature: func Defaults[K comparable, V any](dst map[K]V, sources ...map[K]V) map[K]V
funcs.doc:
Defaults returns a new map seeded with dst and filled in with entries from
sources for any keys that are not already present. Earlier sources take
precedence over later ones. The inputs are not mutated.

funcs.name: DefaultsDeep
funcs.signature: func DefaultsDeep(dst map[string]any, sources ...map[string]any) map[string]any
funcs.doc:
DefaultsDeep is like Defaults but recurses into nested map[string]any values,
filling in missing keys at every level. The inputs are not mutated.

funcs.name: Defer
funcs.signature: func Defer(fn func()) *time.Timer
funcs.doc:
Defer schedules fn to run as soon as possible on a separate goroutine and
returns the underlying *time.Timer, whose Stop method cancels the pending
invocation. It mirrors lodash's defer.

funcs.name: Delay
funcs.signature: func Delay(fn func(), wait time.Duration) *time.Timer
funcs.doc:
Delay schedules fn to run after wait has elapsed and returns the underlying
*time.Timer. Call the timer's Stop method to cancel the pending invocation
before it fires. It mirrors lodash's delay.

funcs.name: Difference
funcs.signature: func Difference[T comparable](s []T, others ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
Difference returns the elements of s that are not present in any of the
others slices, preserving order and de-duplicating.

funcs.name: DifferenceBy
funcs.signature: func DifferenceBy[T any, K comparable](fn func(T) K, s []T, others ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
DifferenceBy returns the elements of s whose key (as produced by fn) does not
appear among the keys of any of the others slices, de-duplicated by key.

funcs.name: DifferenceWith
funcs.signature: func DifferenceWith[T any](eq func(a, b T) bool, s []T, others ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
DifferenceWith returns the elements of s that are not considered equal, by the
eq comparator, to any element of the others slices.

funcs.name: Divide
funcs.signature: func Divide[T Number](a, b T) T
funcs.doc: Divide returns the quotient a divided by b.

funcs.name: Drop
funcs.signature: func Drop[T any](s []T, n int) []T
funcs.doc: Drop returns the slice with the first n elements removed.

funcs.name: DropRight
funcs.signature: func DropRight[T any](s []T, n int) []T
funcs.doc: DropRight returns the slice with the last n elements removed.

funcs.name: DropRightWhile
funcs.signature: func DropRightWhile[T any](s []T, pred func(T) bool) []T
funcs.doc:
DropRightWhile drops the trailing run of elements for which pred returns true
and returns the rest.

funcs.name: DropWhile
funcs.signature: func DropWhile[T any](s []T, pred func(T) bool) []T
funcs.doc:
DropWhile drops the leading run of elements for which pred returns true and
returns the rest.

funcs.name: EndsWith
funcs.signature: func EndsWith(s, suffix string) bool
funcs.doc: EndsWith reports whether s ends with suffix.

funcs.name: Eq
funcs.signature: func Eq[T comparable](a, b T) bool
funcs.doc:
Eq reports whether two comparable values are equal. It is the typed
counterpart to IsEqual for values whose type is known at compile time.

funcs.name: Escape
funcs.signature: func Escape(s string) string
funcs.doc:
Escape converts the characters "&", "<", ">", '"' and "'" in s to their
corresponding HTML entities.

funcs.name: EscapeRegExp
funcs.signature: func EscapeRegExp(s string) string
funcs.doc:
EscapeRegExp escapes the characters that have special meaning in regular
expressions so that s can be matched literally.

funcs.name: Every
funcs.signature: func Every[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) bool
funcs.doc:
Every reports whether fn returns true for every element. It returns true for
an empty slice.

funcs.name: EveryMap
funcs.signature: func EveryMap[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, pred func(V, K) bool) bool
funcs.doc:
EveryMap reports whether pred returns true for every value/key pair in m.
It returns true for an empty map.

funcs.name: Fill
funcs.signature: func Fill[T any](value T, n int) []T
funcs.doc: Fill returns a new slice of length n where every element is value.

funcs.name: FillRange
funcs.signature: func FillRange[T any](s []T, value T, start, end int) []T
funcs.doc:
FillRange returns a copy of s with the elements in the half-open index range
[start, end) replaced by value. Negative indexes count from the end of the
slice, and the range is clamped to the bounds of s. The input slice is not
modified. It mirrors lodash's fill with explicit start and end arguments.

funcs.name: Filter
funcs.signature: func Filter[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) []T
funcs.doc: Filter returns a new slice containing every element for which fn returns true.

funcs.name: FilterMap
funcs.signature: func FilterMap[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, pred func(V, K) bool) []V
funcs.doc:
FilterMap returns the values of m for which pred returns true. The result
order is unspecified.

funcs.name: Find
funcs.signature: func Find[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) (T, bool)
funcs.doc:
Find returns the first element for which fn returns true, along with true.
If no element matches, it returns the zero value and false.

funcs.name: FindIndex
funcs.signature: func FindIndex[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) int
funcs.doc:
FindIndex returns the index of the first element for which fn returns true,
or -1 if none match.

funcs.name: FindKey
funcs.signature: func FindKey[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, pred func(K, V) bool) (K, bool)
funcs.doc:
FindKey returns the first key whose key/value pair satisfies pred, along with
true. Because Go map iteration is unordered, "first" is not deterministic
across runs when multiple keys match. It returns the zero key and false when
nothing matches.

funcs.name: FindLast
funcs.signature: func FindLast[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) (T, bool)
funcs.doc: FindLast returns the last element for which fn returns true.

funcs.name: FindLastIndex
funcs.signature: func FindLastIndex[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) int
funcs.doc:
FindLastIndex returns the index of the last element for which fn returns true,
or -1 if none match.

funcs.name: FindMap
funcs.signature: func FindMap[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, pred func(V, K) bool) (V, bool)
funcs.doc:
FindMap returns a value for which pred returns true, along with true. When
multiple pairs match, which one is returned is unspecified. It returns the
zero value and false when nothing matches.

funcs.name: First
funcs.signature: func First[T any](s []T) (T, bool)
funcs.doc: First is an alias for Head: it returns the first element of the slice.

funcs.name: FlatMap
funcs.signature: func FlatMap[T, R any](s []T, fn func(T) []R) []R
funcs.doc:
FlatMap maps each element to a slice via fn and flattens the results by one
level.

funcs.name: FlatMapDeep
funcs.signature: func FlatMapDeep[T any](s []any, fn func(any) []any) []T
funcs.doc:
FlatMapDeep maps every element of s through fn and then recursively flattens
the collected results to any depth, returning a flat []T. Each result of fn
may itself contain nested []any values, which are flattened as well. It
mirrors lodash's flatMapDeep.

funcs.name: FlatMapDepth
funcs.signature: func FlatMapDepth[T any](s []any, fn func(any) []any, depth int) []T
funcs.doc:
FlatMapDepth maps every element of s through fn and then flattens the
collected results by up to depth levels, returning a flat []T. A depth of 0
performs no flattening. It mirrors lodash's flatMapDepth.

funcs.name: Flatten
funcs.signature: func Flatten[T any](s [][]T) []T
funcs.doc: Flatten flattens a slice of slices by a single level.

funcs.name: FlattenDeep
funcs.signature: func FlattenDeep[T any](s []any) []T
funcs.doc:
FlattenDeep recursively flattens a nested structure into a flat slice. The
nested value must be either a T or a []any of the same shape.

funcs.name: FlattenDepth
funcs.signature: func FlattenDepth[T any](s []any, depth int) []T
funcs.doc:
FlattenDepth flattens a nested structure up to depth levels deep. A depth of 0
returns a copy of the input unchanged; higher depths flatten further.

funcs.name: Flip
funcs.signature: func Flip[A, B, R any](fn func(A, B) R) func(B, A) R
funcs.doc: Flip returns a function that invokes fn with its two arguments swapped.

funcs.name: Floor
funcs.signature: func Floor(value float64, precision int) float64
funcs.doc:
Floor rounds value down to precision decimal places. A negative precision
rounds to the left of the decimal point. Like lodash's _.floor it shifts the
decimal exponent to avoid floating-point error, so Floor(4.1, 2) is exactly
4.1 rather than 4.09.

funcs.name: Flow
funcs.signature: func Flow[T any](fns ...func(T) T) func(T) T
funcs.doc:
Flow composes the given same-typed transforms into a single function that
applies them left to right: the output of each becomes the input of the next.
Flow with no functions returns the identity function.

funcs.name: FlowRight
funcs.signature: func FlowRight[T any](fns ...func(T) T) func(T) T
funcs.doc:
FlowRight composes the given same-typed transforms into a single function that
applies them right to left (mathematical composition).

funcs.name: ForEach
funcs.signature: func ForEach[T any](s []T, fn func(T))
funcs.doc: ForEach invokes fn once for each element of the slice.

funcs.name: ForEachI
funcs.signature: func ForEachI[T any](s []T, fn func(T, int))
funcs.doc: ForEachI invokes fn once for each element, passing the element and its index.

funcs.name: ForEachRight
funcs.signature: func ForEachRight[T any](s []T, fn func(T))
funcs.doc: ForEachRight invokes fn once for each element from right to left.

funcs.name: ForOwn
funcs.signature: func ForOwn[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, fn func(K, V))
funcs.doc:
ForOwn invokes fn for each key/value pair of the map. Iteration order is
unspecified.

funcs.name: FromEntries
funcs.signature: func FromEntries[K comparable, V any](entries []Entry[K, V]) map[K]V
funcs.doc: FromEntries builds a map from a slice of entries.

funcs.name: FromPairs
funcs.signature: func FromPairs[K comparable, V any](pairs []Pair[K, V]) map[K]V
funcs.doc:
FromPairs builds a map from a slice of key/value Pair values. Later pairs
overwrite earlier ones with the same key.

funcs.name: Get
funcs.signature: func Get(m map[string]any, path string) (any, bool)
funcs.doc:
Get retrieves a nested value from a map[string]any using a property path.
The path may use dotted and bracket notation (see ToPath) and may traverse
[]any slices via integer indices. The second return value reports whether the
full path resolved to a value.

funcs.name: GetOr
funcs.signature: func GetOr(m map[string]any, path string, fallback any) any
funcs.doc: GetOr is like Get but returns fallback when the path does not resolve.

funcs.name: GroupBy
funcs.signature: func GroupBy[T any, K comparable](s []T, fn func(T) K) map[K][]T
funcs.doc: GroupBy groups the slice elements into a map keyed by the result of fn.

funcs.name: GroupByMap
funcs.signature: func GroupByMap[K comparable, V any, G comparable](m map[K]V, fn func(V) G) map[G][]V
funcs.doc:
GroupByMap groups the values of m by the key returned by fn, producing a map
from group key to the slice of values in that group. Element order within
each group is unspecified.

funcs.name: Gt
funcs.signature: func Gt[T Ordered](a, b T) bool
funcs.doc: Gt reports whether a is greater than b.

funcs.name: Gte
funcs.signature: func Gte[T Ordered](a, b T) bool
funcs.doc: Gte reports whether a is greater than or equal to b.

funcs.name: Has
funcs.signature: func Has(m map[string]any, path string) bool
funcs.doc: Has reports whether path resolves to a value in the nested map.

funcs.name: Head
funcs.signature: func Head[T any](s []T) (T, bool)
funcs.doc:
Head returns the first element of the slice and true, or the zero value and
false when the slice is empty.

funcs.name: Identity
funcs.signature: func Identity[T any](value T) T
funcs.doc: Identity returns its argument unchanged. It is useful as a default transform.

funcs.name: InRange
funcs.signature: func InRange[T Number](value, lower, upper T) bool
funcs.doc:
InRange reports whether value lies within the half-open interval [lower, upper).
If lower is greater than upper the bounds are swapped.

funcs.name: Includes
funcs.signature: func Includes[T comparable](s []T, value T) bool
funcs.doc: Includes reports whether value is present in the slice.

funcs.name: IncludesValue
funcs.signature: func IncludesValue[K, V comparable](m map[K]V, value V) bool
funcs.doc:
IncludesValue reports whether value is one of the values stored in m. It
mirrors lodash's _.includes applied to an object.

funcs.name: IndexOf
funcs.signature: func IndexOf[T comparable](s []T, value T) int
funcs.doc: IndexOf returns the index of the first occurrence of value, or -1.

funcs.name: IndexOfFrom
funcs.signature: func IndexOfFrom[T comparable](s []T, value T, from int) int
funcs.doc:
IndexOfFrom returns the index of the first occurrence of value at or after
index from, or -1 if it is absent. A negative from counts from the end.

funcs.name: Initial
funcs.signature: func Initial[T any](s []T) []T
funcs.doc: Initial returns a new slice containing all but the last element.

funcs.name: Intersection
funcs.signature: func Intersection[T comparable](slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
Intersection returns the unique elements common to all provided slices,
ordered by their first appearance in the first slice.

funcs.name: IntersectionBy
funcs.signature: func IntersectionBy[T any, K comparable](fn func(T) K, slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
IntersectionBy returns the elements present (by key) in every provided slice,
ordered by first appearance in the first slice and de-duplicated by key.

funcs.name: IntersectionWith
funcs.signature: func IntersectionWith[T any](eq func(a, b T) bool, slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
IntersectionWith returns the elements of the first slice that are equal, by
the eq comparator, to some element of every other slice.

funcs.name: Invert
funcs.signature: func Invert[K comparable, V comparable](m map[K]V) map[V]K
funcs.doc:
Invert returns a new map with keys and values swapped. When multiple keys map
to the same value, later iterations overwrite earlier ones.

funcs.name: InvertBy
funcs.signature: func InvertBy[K comparable, V any, G comparable](m map[K]V, fn func(V) G) map[G][]K
funcs.doc:
InvertBy groups the keys of m by the group value produced by fn from each
value, returning a map from group to the slice of keys that produced it.

funcs.name: IsArrayLike
funcs.signature: func IsArrayLike(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsArrayLike reports whether value has a length: a slice, array or string.
Functions and maps are not array-like. It mirrors lodash's isArrayLike.

funcs.name: IsArrayLikeObject
funcs.signature: func IsArrayLikeObject(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsArrayLikeObject reports whether value is array-like and also an object,
i.e. a slice or array but not a string. It mirrors lodash's
isArrayLikeObject.

funcs.name: IsBool
funcs.signature: func IsBool(value any) bool
funcs.doc: IsBool reports whether value's dynamic type is a boolean.

funcs.name: IsBuffer
funcs.signature: func IsBuffer(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsBuffer reports whether value is a byte slice ([]byte), the closest Go
analogue of a Node.js Buffer.

funcs.name: IsDate
funcs.signature: func IsDate(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsDate reports whether value is a time.Time (or a non-nil *time.Time),
the closest Go analogue of a JavaScript Date.

funcs.name: IsEmpty
funcs.signature: func IsEmpty(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsEmpty reports whether value is empty. Nil, empty strings, and empty maps,
slices, arrays and channels are considered empty. Following lodash semantics,
numbers, booleans and other scalar values are always reported as empty.

funcs.name: IsEqual
funcs.signature: func IsEqual(a, b any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsEqual reports whether a and b are deeply equal, recursively comparing the
contents of maps, slices, arrays, structs and pointers. It is a thin,
well-defined wrapper over reflect.DeepEqual.

funcs.name: IsEqualWith
funcs.signature: func IsEqualWith(a, b any, customizer func(a, b any) (equal bool, handled bool)) bool
funcs.doc:
IsEqualWith performs a deep equality comparison like IsEqual, but first
consults customizer. When customizer's second return value (handled) is true,
its first return value is used as the comparison result; otherwise the
default deep comparison is applied.

funcs.name: IsError
funcs.signature: func IsError(value any) bool
funcs.doc: IsError reports whether value implements the error interface and is non-nil.

funcs.name: IsFinite
funcs.signature: func IsFinite(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsFinite reports whether value is a finite number (not NaN and not an
infinity). Non-numeric values are not finite.

funcs.name: IsFunction
funcs.signature: func IsFunction(value any) bool
funcs.doc: IsFunction reports whether value is a function.

funcs.name: IsInteger
funcs.signature: func IsInteger(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsInteger reports whether value is an integer-valued number: an integer type,
or a float with no fractional part.

funcs.name: IsLength
funcs.signature: func IsLength(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsLength reports whether value is a valid array-like length: an integer-valued
number that is non-negative and no greater than 2^53-1.

funcs.name: IsMap
funcs.signature: func IsMap(value any) bool
funcs.doc: IsMap reports whether value's dynamic type is a map.

funcs.name: IsMatch
funcs.signature: func IsMatch(object, source map[string]any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsMatch reports whether object contains all of the key/value pairs in source,
comparing nested map[string]any values recursively. Extra keys in object are
ignored.

funcs.name: IsMatchWith
funcs.signature: func IsMatchWith(object, source map[string]any, customizer func(objValue, srcValue any) bool) bool
funcs.doc:
IsMatchWith reports whether object matches the shape of source using a custom
comparator for each source key. Every key in source must be present in object
and satisfy customizer(objectValue, sourceValue). An empty source always
matches.

funcs.name: IsNaN
funcs.signature: func IsNaN(value any) bool
funcs.doc: IsNaN reports whether value is the floating-point Not-a-Number value.

funcs.name: IsNil
funcs.signature: func IsNil(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsNil reports whether value is nil, including typed nil pointers, maps,
slices, channels, functions and interfaces.

funcs.name: IsNull
funcs.signature: func IsNull(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsNull reports whether value is nil, either as an untyped nil interface or as
a typed nil pointer, map, slice, channel or function. It is the Go analogue
of a JavaScript null check.

funcs.name: IsNumber
funcs.signature: func IsNumber(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsNumber reports whether value's dynamic type is any built-in numeric type
(integer, unsigned, or floating point).

funcs.name: IsObject
funcs.signature: func IsObject(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsObject reports whether value is a non-primitive, non-nil value: a map,
slice, array, struct, pointer, channel or function. Strings, booleans and
numbers return false. It mirrors lodash's isObject.

funcs.name: IsObjectLike
funcs.signature: func IsObjectLike(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsObjectLike reports whether value is non-nil and of a composite kind: a map,
slice, array, struct or pointer.

funcs.name: IsPlainObject
funcs.signature: func IsPlainObject(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsPlainObject reports whether value is a plain map keyed by strings, i.e. the
Go analogue of a JavaScript plain object such as map[string]any.

funcs.name: IsRegExp
funcs.signature: func IsRegExp(value any) bool
funcs.doc: IsRegExp reports whether value is a compiled *regexp.Regexp.

funcs.name: IsSafeInteger
funcs.signature: func IsSafeInteger(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsSafeInteger reports whether value is an integer-valued number that can be
represented exactly as a float64, i.e. within the inclusive range
[-(2^53-1), 2^53-1]. It mirrors lodash's isSafeInteger.

funcs.name: IsSlice
funcs.signature: func IsSlice(value any) bool
funcs.doc: IsSlice reports whether value's dynamic type is a slice.

funcs.name: IsString
funcs.signature: func IsString(value any) bool
funcs.doc: IsString reports whether value's dynamic type is a string.

funcs.name: IsUndefined
funcs.signature: func IsUndefined(value any) bool
funcs.doc:
IsUndefined reports whether value is an untyped nil interface. Unlike IsNull
it does not consider typed nil values (such as a nil *T) to be undefined,
mirroring the distinction JavaScript draws between undefined and null.

funcs.name: Join
funcs.signature: func Join[T any](s []T, sep string) string
funcs.doc:
Join concatenates the string forms of the elements using sep. Elements are
rendered with ToString.

funcs.name: KebabCase
funcs.signature: func KebabCase(s string) string
funcs.doc: KebabCase converts a string to kebab-case.

funcs.name: KeyBy
funcs.signature: func KeyBy[T any, K comparable](s []T, fn func(T) K) map[K]T
funcs.doc:
KeyBy builds a map keyed by the result of fn. When multiple elements produce
the same key, later elements overwrite earlier ones.

funcs.name: Keys
funcs.signature: func Keys[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V) []K
funcs.doc: Keys returns the keys of the map in unspecified order.

funcs.name: Last
funcs.signature: func Last[T any](s []T) (T, bool)
funcs.doc:
Last returns the final element of the slice and true, or the zero value and
false when the slice is empty.

funcs.name: LastIndexOf
funcs.signature: func LastIndexOf[T comparable](s []T, value T) int
funcs.doc: LastIndexOf returns the index of the last occurrence of value, or -1.

funcs.name: LastIndexOfFrom
funcs.signature: func LastIndexOfFrom[T comparable](s []T, value T, from int) int
funcs.doc:
LastIndexOfFrom returns the index of the last occurrence of value at or before
index from, or -1 if it is absent. A negative from counts from the end.

funcs.name: LowerCase
funcs.signature: func LowerCase(s string) string
funcs.doc:
LowerCase converts s to space-separated lower case words, splitting on case,
digit and separator boundaries. Like lodash's _.lowerCase it deburrs Latin
letters and removes contraction apostrophes before splitting (see
compoundWords), so e.g. LowerCase("À") yields "a".

funcs.name: LowerFirst
funcs.signature: func LowerFirst(s string) string
funcs.doc:
LowerFirst lower-cases the first character of the string, leaving the rest
unchanged.

funcs.name: Lt
funcs.signature: func Lt[T Ordered](a, b T) bool
funcs.doc: Lt reports whether a is less than b.

funcs.name: Lte
funcs.signature: func Lte[T Ordered](a, b T) bool
funcs.doc: Lte reports whether a is less than or equal to b.

funcs.name: Map
funcs.signature: func Map[T, R any](s []T, fn func(T) R) []R
funcs.doc:
Map returns a new slice populated with the results of calling fn on every
element of the input slice. The callback receives each element.

funcs.name: MapI
funcs.signature: func MapI[T, R any](s []T, fn func(T, int) R) []R
funcs.doc: MapI is like Map but the callback also receives the element index.

funcs.name: MapKeys
funcs.signature: func MapKeys[K comparable, V any, K2 comparable](m map[K]V, fn func(K, V) K2) map[K2]V
funcs.doc:
MapKeys returns a new map whose keys are produced by applying fn to each
original key/value pair.

funcs.name: MapToSlice
funcs.signature: func MapToSlice[K comparable, V, R any](m map[K]V, fn func(V, K) R) []R
funcs.doc:
MapToSlice applies fn to every value/key pair in m and returns the results as
a slice. The result order is unspecified. It mirrors lodash's _.map applied
to an object.

funcs.name: MapValues
funcs.signature: func MapValues[K comparable, V any, V2 any](m map[K]V, fn func(K, V) V2) map[K]V2
funcs.doc:
MapValues returns a new map whose values are produced by applying fn to each
original key/value pair.

funcs.name: Matches
funcs.signature: func Matches(source map[string]any) func(map[string]any) bool
funcs.doc:
Matches returns a predicate that reports whether its argument contains all of
the key/value pairs in source, using IsMatch semantics.

funcs.name: MatchesProperty
funcs.signature: func MatchesProperty(path string, srcValue any) func(map[string]any) bool
funcs.doc:
MatchesProperty returns a predicate that reports whether the value found at
path (see ToPath) within a map[string]any is deeply equal to srcValue. It is
the property-path analogue of Matches and mirrors lodash's matchesProperty.

funcs.name: Max
funcs.signature: func Max[T cmp.Ordered](s []T) (T, bool)
funcs.doc:
Max returns the largest element and true, or the zero value and false when
the slice is empty.

funcs.name: MaxBy
funcs.signature: func MaxBy[T any, K cmp.Ordered](s []T, fn func(T) K) (T, bool)
funcs.doc: MaxBy returns the element whose key (as produced by fn) is largest.

funcs.name: MaxByMap
funcs.signature: func MaxByMap[K comparable, V any, R cmp.Ordered](m map[K]V, fn func(V) R) (V, bool)
funcs.doc:
MaxByMap returns the value of m for which fn yields the largest ordered key,
along with true, or the zero value and false when m is empty. Ties are broken
arbitrarily.

funcs.name: Mean
funcs.signature: func Mean[T Number](s []T) float64
funcs.doc:
Mean returns the arithmetic mean of the elements as a float64. It returns 0
for an empty slice.

funcs.name: MeanBy
funcs.signature: func MeanBy[T any, R Number](s []T, fn func(T) R) float64
funcs.doc: MeanBy returns the mean of applying fn to each element as a float64.

funcs.name: Memoize
funcs.signature: func Memoize[K comparable, R any](fn func(K) R) func(K) R
funcs.doc:
Memoize returns a function that caches the result of fn for each distinct
argument. The cache is safe for concurrent use.

funcs.name: MemoizeBy
funcs.signature: func MemoizeBy[A any, K comparable, R any](fn func(A) R, keyFn func(A) K) func(A) R
funcs.doc:
MemoizeBy is like Memoize but derives the cache key from arg via keyFn,
allowing memoization over non-comparable argument types.

funcs.name: Merge
funcs.signature: func Merge[K comparable, V any](maps ...map[K]V) map[K]V
funcs.doc:
Merge returns a new map combining all provided maps. Later maps override
earlier ones for duplicate keys.

funcs.name: MergeWith
funcs.signature: func MergeWith[K comparable, V any](fn func(existing, incoming V) V, maps ...map[K]V) map[K]V
funcs.doc:
MergeWith combines all provided maps into a new map. When a key appears in
more than one map, the resolver fn decides the merged value from the existing
(accumulated) value and the incoming one.

funcs.name: Min
funcs.signature: func Min[T cmp.Ordered](s []T) (T, bool)
funcs.doc:
Min returns the smallest element and true, or the zero value and false when
the slice is empty.

funcs.name: MinBy
funcs.signature: func MinBy[T any, K cmp.Ordered](s []T, fn func(T) K) (T, bool)
funcs.doc: MinBy returns the element whose key (as produced by fn) is smallest.

funcs.name: MinByMap
funcs.signature: func MinByMap[K comparable, V any, R cmp.Ordered](m map[K]V, fn func(V) R) (V, bool)
funcs.doc:
MinByMap returns the value of m for which fn yields the smallest ordered key,
along with true, or the zero value and false when m is empty. Ties are broken
arbitrarily.

funcs.name: Multiply
funcs.signature: func Multiply[T Number](a, b T) T
funcs.doc: Multiply returns the product of a and b.

funcs.name: Negate
funcs.signature: func Negate[T any](pred func(T) bool) func(T) bool
funcs.doc: Negate returns a predicate that reports the logical negation of pred.

funcs.name: None
funcs.signature: func None[T any](s []T, pred func(T) bool) bool
funcs.doc:
None reports whether pred returns false for every element. It is the negation
of Some and returns true for an empty slice.

funcs.name: NoneMap
funcs.signature: func NoneMap[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, pred func(V, K) bool) bool
funcs.doc:
NoneMap reports whether pred returns false for every value/key pair in m. It
returns true for an empty map.

funcs.name: Noop
funcs.signature: func Noop()
funcs.doc: Noop does nothing. It is handy as a default callback.

funcs.name: Nth
funcs.signature: func Nth[T any](s []T, n int) (T, bool)
funcs.doc:
Nth returns the element at index n and true. Negative indices count from the
end (-1 is the last element). An out-of-range index yields the zero value and
false.

funcs.name: NthArg
funcs.signature: func NthArg[T any](n int) func(...T) T
funcs.doc:
NthArg returns a function that returns the argument at position n from its
variadic arguments. A negative n counts from the end. When n is out of range
the zero value is returned.

funcs.name: Omit
funcs.signature: func Omit[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, keys ...K) map[K]V
funcs.doc: Omit returns a new map with the given keys removed.

funcs.name: OmitBy
funcs.signature: func OmitBy[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, fn func(K, V) bool) map[K]V
funcs.doc: OmitBy returns a new map excluding entries for which fn returns true.

funcs.name: Once
funcs.signature: func Once[R any](fn func() R) func() R
funcs.doc:
Once returns a function that invokes fn at most once. Subsequent calls return
the value produced by the first invocation. It is safe for concurrent use.

funcs.name: OrderBy
funcs.signature: func OrderBy[T any](s []T, keys []func(a, b T) int, orders []Order) []T
funcs.doc:
OrderBy returns a new slice sorted by multiple key functions with per-key sort
directions. The keys are compared in order until a difference is found. The
number of orders may be shorter than keys; missing directions default to Asc.

funcs.name: Over
funcs.signature: func Over[T, R any](fns ...func(T) R) func(T) []R
funcs.doc:
Over returns a function that runs its input through every transform in fns and
collects the results into a slice, preserving order.

funcs.name: OverArgs
funcs.signature: func OverArgs[T, R any](fn func(...T) R, transforms ...func(T) T) func(...T) R
funcs.doc:
OverArgs returns a function that transforms its variadic arguments with the
corresponding transform in transforms (by position) before passing them to
fn. Arguments without a matching transform are passed through unchanged.

funcs.name: OverEvery
funcs.signature: func OverEvery[T any](preds ...func(T) bool) func(T) bool
funcs.doc:
OverEvery returns a predicate that reports whether the input satisfies every
predicate in preds. With no predicates it always returns true.

funcs.name: OverSome
funcs.signature: func OverSome[T any](preds ...func(T) bool) func(T) bool
funcs.doc:
OverSome returns a predicate that reports whether the input satisfies at least
one predicate in preds. With no predicates it always returns false.

funcs.name: Pad
funcs.signature: func Pad(s string, length int, chars string) string
funcs.doc:
Pad pads a string on both sides with chars until it reaches length. If the
string is already at least length long it is returned unchanged.

funcs.name: PadEnd
funcs.signature: func PadEnd(s string, length int, chars string) string
funcs.doc: PadEnd pads a string on the right with chars until it reaches length.

funcs.name: PadStart
funcs.signature: func PadStart(s string, length int, chars string) string
funcs.doc: PadStart pads a string on the left with chars until it reaches length.

funcs.name: ParseFloat
funcs.signature: func ParseFloat(s string) (float64, error)
funcs.doc:
ParseFloat parses s as a floating-point number and returns the result as a
float64. Surrounding whitespace is ignored. It complements ParseInt for
fractional input, delegating to strconv.ParseFloat.

funcs.name: ParseInt
funcs.signature: func ParseInt(s string, base int) (int64, error)
funcs.doc:
ParseInt parses s as an integer in the given base and returns the result as
an int64. Surrounding whitespace is ignored. A base of 0 infers the base from
any "0x", "0o" or "0b" prefix (defaulting to decimal). It mirrors lodash's
parseInt, delegating to strconv.ParseInt for the conversion.

funcs.name: Partial
funcs.signature: func Partial[A, B, R any](fn func(A, B) R, a A) func(B) R
funcs.doc:
Partial binds the first argument of a two-argument function, returning a unary
function of the remaining argument.

funcs.name: PartialRight
funcs.signature: func PartialRight[A, B, R any](fn func(A, B) R, b B) func(A) R
funcs.doc:
PartialRight binds the last argument of a two-argument function, returning a
unary function of the remaining (first) argument.

funcs.name: Partition
funcs.signature: func Partition[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) (truthy, falsy []T)
funcs.doc:
Partition splits the slice into two slices: elements for which fn is true and
elements for which it is false.

funcs.name: PartitionMap
funcs.signature: func PartitionMap[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, pred func(V, K) bool) (truthy, falsy []V)
funcs.doc:
PartitionMap splits the values of m into two slices: truthy holds the values
for which pred returns true and falsy holds the rest. Element order within
each slice is unspecified.

funcs.name: PascalCase
funcs.signature: func PascalCase(s string) string
funcs.doc: PascalCase converts a string to PascalCase (a.k.a. UpperCamelCase).

funcs.name: Pick
funcs.signature: func Pick[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, keys ...K) map[K]V
funcs.doc: Pick returns a new map containing only the given keys that are present in m.

funcs.name: PickBy
funcs.signature: func PickBy[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, fn func(K, V) bool) map[K]V
funcs.doc: PickBy returns a new map containing entries for which fn returns true.

funcs.name: Property
funcs.signature: func Property(path string) func(map[string]any) (any, bool)
funcs.doc:
Property returns a function that reads the value at path from a nested
map[string]any using Get semantics (dotted and bracketed paths).

funcs.name: PropertyOf
funcs.signature: func PropertyOf(object map[string]any) func(string) (any, bool)
funcs.doc:
PropertyOf returns a function that reads the value at a given path from the
bound object using Get semantics.

funcs.name: Pull
funcs.signature: func Pull[T comparable](s []T, values ...T) []T
funcs.doc:
Pull returns a new slice with every occurrence of the given values removed.
It is the variadic form of PullAll.

funcs.name: PullAll
funcs.signature: func PullAll[T comparable](s []T, values []T) []T
funcs.doc:
PullAll returns a new slice with every occurrence of the given values removed.
The input is not mutated (unlike lodash's mutating _.pullAll).

funcs.name: PullAllBy
funcs.signature: func PullAllBy[T any, K comparable](s []T, values []T, fn func(T) K) []T
funcs.doc: PullAllBy is like PullAll but elements are matched by the key produced by fn.

funcs.name: PullAllWith
funcs.signature: func PullAllWith[T any](s []T, values []T, eq func(a, b T) bool) []T
funcs.doc:
PullAllWith returns a new slice with every element of s that is equal to any
element of values removed, using eq for comparison. The input slice is not
modified. It is the comparator-based analogue of PullAll.

funcs.name: PullAt
funcs.signature: func PullAt[T any](s []T, indexes ...int) []T
funcs.doc:
PullAt returns a new slice with the elements at the given indices removed.
Negative and out-of-range indices are ignored. The input is not mutated.

funcs.name: Random
funcs.signature: func Random(min, max int, rng *rand.Rand) int
funcs.doc:
Random returns a pseudo-random integer in the inclusive range [min, max]
drawn from rng, so results are deterministic when rng is seeded. If min is
greater than max the bounds are swapped.

funcs.name: RandomFloat
funcs.signature: func RandomFloat(min, max float64, rng *rand.Rand) float64
funcs.doc:
RandomFloat returns a pseudo-random float64 in the half-open range [min, max)
drawn from rng. If min is greater than max the bounds are swapped.

funcs.name: Range
funcs.signature: func Range(end int) []int
funcs.doc:
Range returns a slice of integers from 0 up to (but not including) end. If end
is negative it counts down.

funcs.name: RangeRight
funcs.signature: func RangeRight(end int) []int
funcs.doc: RangeRight is like Range but the returned integers are in descending order.

funcs.name: RangeStep
funcs.signature: func RangeStep(start, end, step int) []int
funcs.doc:
RangeStep returns a slice of integers from start up to (but not including) end
advancing by step. The step must be non-zero; the direction is inferred from
the sign of (end - start) when step's sign disagrees.

funcs.name: Rearg
funcs.signature: func Rearg[T, R any](fn func(...T) R, indexes ...int) func(...T) R
funcs.doc:
Rearg returns a function that reorders its variadic arguments according to
indexes before calling fn: the i-th argument passed to fn is the argument at
position indexes[i] of the call. Out-of-range indexes contribute the zero
value.

funcs.name: Reduce
funcs.signature: func Reduce[T, A any](s []T, fn func(acc A, cur T) A, init A) A
funcs.doc:
Reduce boils the slice down to a single accumulated value by iterating from
left to right, seeded with init.

funcs.name: ReduceMap
funcs.signature: func ReduceMap[K comparable, V, A any](m map[K]V, fn func(acc A, v V, k K) A, init A) A
funcs.doc:
ReduceMap boils m down to a single accumulated value by applying fn to each
value/key pair, seeded with init. Because iteration order is unspecified, fn
should be associative and commutative for a deterministic result.

funcs.name: ReduceRight
funcs.signature: func ReduceRight[T, A any](s []T, fn func(acc A, cur T) A, init A) A
funcs.doc: ReduceRight is like Reduce but iterates from right to left.

funcs.name: Reject
funcs.signature: func Reject[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) []T
funcs.doc: Reject is the opposite of Filter: it keeps elements for which fn returns false.

funcs.name: RejectMap
funcs.signature: func RejectMap[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, pred func(V, K) bool) []V
funcs.doc:
RejectMap returns the values of m for which pred returns false. The result
order is unspecified.

funcs.name: Remove
funcs.signature: func Remove[T any](s []T, pred func(T) bool) []T
funcs.doc:
Remove returns a new slice containing only the elements for which pred returns
false, i.e. the elements that lodash's mutating _.remove would leave behind.
The input is not mutated.

funcs.name: Repeat
funcs.signature: func Repeat(s string, n int) string
funcs.doc: Repeat returns a string consisting of n copies of s.

funcs.name: Replace
funcs.signature: func Replace(s, old, replacement string) string
funcs.doc:
Replace returns s with the first non-overlapping occurrence of old replaced by
replacement.

funcs.name: Rest
funcs.signature: func Rest[T, R any](fn func([]T) R) func(...T) R
funcs.doc:
Rest wraps fn, which takes a single slice argument, so that it can be called
with variadic arguments instead. It is the inverse of Spread and mirrors
lodash's rest with the collected arguments passed as one slice.

funcs.name: Result
funcs.signature: func Result(m map[string]any, path string) (any, bool)
funcs.doc:
Result is like Get but, when the resolved value is a func() any, it invokes
that function and returns its result. This mirrors lodash's _.result.

funcs.name: Reverse
funcs.signature: func Reverse[T any](s []T) []T
funcs.doc:
Reverse returns a new slice with the elements in reverse order. The input is
not mutated.

funcs.name: Round
funcs.signature: func Round(value float64, precision int) float64
funcs.doc:
Round rounds value to precision decimal places using round-half-away-from-zero.
A negative precision rounds to the left of the decimal point. Like lodash's
_.round it shifts the decimal exponent rather than scaling by a power of ten,
so Round(4.006, 2) is exactly 4.01.

funcs.name: Sample
funcs.signature: func Sample[T any](s []T, rng *rand.Rand) (T, bool)
funcs.doc:
Sample returns a single random element from the slice using the provided
random source, making it deterministic when seeded. The second return value
is false when the slice is empty.

funcs.name: SampleN
funcs.signature: func SampleN[T any](s []T, n int, rng *rand.Rand) []T
funcs.doc:
SampleN returns n unique random elements from the slice using the provided
random source. If n exceeds the length, a shuffled copy of the whole slice is
returned.

funcs.name: Set
funcs.signature: func Set(m map[string]any, path string, value any) map[string]any
funcs.doc:
Set returns a copy of m with the value at path set to value, creating
intermediate maps as needed. The input map is not mutated; only the
containers along the path are cloned (copy-on-write).

funcs.name: SetWith
funcs.signature: func SetWith(m map[string]any, path string, value any, customizer func(key string) any) map[string]any
funcs.doc:
SetWith returns a copy of m with value assigned at path, using customizer to
create any intermediate containers that do not yet exist. For each missing
segment, customizer is called with the key of the segment about to be
created; if it returns a non-nil map[string]any that container is used,
otherwise a fresh map[string]any is created. The input map is not mutated. It
mirrors lodash's setWith.

funcs.name: Shuffle
funcs.signature: func Shuffle[T any](s []T, rng *rand.Rand) []T
funcs.doc:
Shuffle returns a new slice with the elements randomly reordered using the
provided random source. The input is not mutated.

funcs.name: Size
funcs.signature: func Size(collection any) int
funcs.doc:
Size returns the number of elements in collection, which may be a map, slice,
array, string or channel. Any other type reports 0.

funcs.name: Slice
funcs.signature: func Slice[T any](s []T, start, end int) []T
funcs.doc:
Slice returns the portion of s between start (inclusive) and end (exclusive).
Negative indices count from the end. Out-of-range bounds are clamped. The
input is not mutated.

funcs.name: SnakeCase
funcs.signature: func SnakeCase(s string) string
funcs.doc: SnakeCase converts a string to snake_case.

funcs.name: Some
funcs.signature: func Some[T any](s []T, fn func(T) bool) bool
funcs.doc:
Some reports whether fn returns true for at least one element. It returns
false for an empty slice.

funcs.name: SomeMap
funcs.signature: func SomeMap[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, pred func(V, K) bool) bool
funcs.doc:
SomeMap reports whether pred returns true for at least one value/key pair in
m. It returns false for an empty map.

funcs.name: SortBy
funcs.signature: func SortBy[T any, K cmp.Ordered](s []T, fn func(T) K) []T
funcs.doc:
SortBy returns a new slice sorted in ascending order by the key returned by
fn. The sort is stable and the input is not mutated.

funcs.name: SortedIndex
funcs.signature: func SortedIndex[T cmp.Ordered](s []T, value T) int
funcs.doc:
SortedIndex returns the lowest index at which value can be inserted into the
sorted slice s to keep it sorted.

funcs.name: SortedIndexBy
funcs.signature: func SortedIndexBy[T any, K cmp.Ordered](s []T, value T, fn func(T) K) int
funcs.doc:
SortedIndexBy returns the lowest insertion index for value in s, comparing
elements by the key produced by fn.

funcs.name: SortedIndexOf
funcs.signature: func SortedIndexOf[T cmp.Ordered](s []T, value T) int
funcs.doc:
SortedIndexOf returns the index of the first occurrence of value in the sorted
slice s using binary search, or -1 if it is absent.

funcs.name: SortedKeys
funcs.signature: func SortedKeys[K cmp.Ordered, V any](m map[K]V) []K
funcs.doc: SortedKeys returns the keys of the map sorted in ascending order.

funcs.name: SortedLastIndex
funcs.signature: func SortedLastIndex[T cmp.Ordered](s []T, value T) int
funcs.doc:
SortedLastIndex returns the highest index at which value can be inserted into
the sorted slice s to keep it sorted.

funcs.name: SortedLastIndexBy
funcs.signature: func SortedLastIndexBy[T any, K cmp.Ordered](s []T, value T, fn func(T) K) int
funcs.doc:
SortedLastIndexBy returns the highest insertion index for value in s,
comparing elements by the key produced by fn.

funcs.name: SortedLastIndexOf
funcs.signature: func SortedLastIndexOf[T cmp.Ordered](s []T, value T) int
funcs.doc:
SortedLastIndexOf returns the index of the last occurrence of value in the
sorted slice s using binary search, or -1 if it is absent.

funcs.name: SortedUniq
funcs.signature: func SortedUniq[T comparable](s []T) []T
funcs.doc:
SortedUniq removes duplicate values from a sorted slice, keeping one of each
run of equal elements. The input is not mutated.

funcs.name: SortedUniqBy
funcs.signature: func SortedUniqBy[T any, K comparable](s []T, fn func(T) K) []T
funcs.doc:
SortedUniqBy removes duplicates from a sorted slice, comparing elements by the
key produced by fn. The input is not mutated.

funcs.name: Split
funcs.signature: func Split(s, sep string, limit int) []string
funcs.doc:
Split divides s around each instance of sep. When limit is greater than zero
at most limit substrings are returned (the final one holding the unsplit
remainder is dropped beyond the limit); a non-positive limit returns all
substrings.

funcs.name: Spread
funcs.signature: func Spread[T, R any](fn func(...T) R) func([]T) R
funcs.doc:
Spread converts a variadic function into one that takes its arguments as a
single slice.

funcs.name: StartCase
funcs.signature: func StartCase(s string) string
funcs.doc:
StartCase converts a string to Start Case, upper-casing the first letter of
each word and separating words with single spaces. Unlike Capitalize it does
not lower-case the remaining letters, so acronyms such as "FOO" are
preserved. This mirrors lodash's _.startCase, which composes upperFirst over
each detected word (e.g. "__FOO_BAR__" -> "FOO BAR").

funcs.name: StartsWith
funcs.signature: func StartsWith(s, prefix string) bool
funcs.doc: StartsWith reports whether s begins with prefix.

funcs.name: StubArray
funcs.signature: func StubArray[T any]() []T
funcs.doc: StubArray returns a new empty slice of T on every call.

funcs.name: StubFalse
funcs.signature: func StubFalse() bool
funcs.doc: StubFalse always returns false.

funcs.name: StubObject
funcs.signature: func StubObject() map[string]any
funcs.doc: StubObject returns a new empty map[string]any on every call.

funcs.name: StubString
funcs.signature: func StubString() string
funcs.doc: StubString returns the empty string.

funcs.name: StubTrue
funcs.signature: func StubTrue() bool
funcs.doc: StubTrue always returns true.

funcs.name: Subtract
funcs.signature: func Subtract[T Number](a, b T) T
funcs.doc: Subtract returns the difference a minus b.

funcs.name: Sum
funcs.signature: func Sum[T Number](s []T) T
funcs.doc:
Sum returns the sum of all elements. It returns the zero value for an empty
slice.

funcs.name: SumBy
funcs.signature: func SumBy[T any, R Number](s []T, fn func(T) R) R
funcs.doc: SumBy returns the sum of applying fn to each element.

funcs.name: Tail
funcs.signature: func Tail[T any](s []T) []T
funcs.doc: Tail returns a new slice containing all but the first element.

funcs.name: Take
funcs.signature: func Take[T any](s []T, n int) []T
funcs.doc:
Take returns the first n elements of the slice. If n exceeds the length, the
whole slice is returned. Negative n yields an empty slice.

funcs.name: TakeRight
funcs.signature: func TakeRight[T any](s []T, n int) []T
funcs.doc: TakeRight returns the last n elements of the slice.

funcs.name: TakeRightWhile
funcs.signature: func TakeRightWhile[T any](s []T, pred func(T) bool) []T
funcs.doc:
TakeRightWhile returns the trailing run of elements for which pred returns
true, in their original order.

funcs.name: TakeWhile
funcs.signature: func TakeWhile[T any](s []T, pred func(T) bool) []T
funcs.doc: TakeWhile returns the leading run of elements for which pred returns true.

funcs.name: Template
funcs.signature: func Template(tmpl string, opts ...TemplateOptions) func(data map[string]any) string
funcs.doc:
Template compiles a text template into a renderer function. Within the
template, "<%= path %>" interpolates the value at path from the data map,
"<%- path %>" interpolates it HTML-escaped, and "<% ... %>" evaluate blocks
are recognized and removed (they are not executed, since no scripting engine
is embedded). Paths are resolved with Get, so dotted and bracket notation are
supported.

funcs.name: Times
funcs.signature: func Times[R any](n int, fn func(int) R) []R
funcs.doc:
Times invokes fn n times, collecting the results into a slice. The call index
(0-based) is passed to fn. A non-positive n yields an empty slice.

funcs.name: ToArray
funcs.signature: func ToArray[T any](s []T) []T
funcs.doc:
ToArray returns a copy of the given slice. It is the slice-typed analogue of
lodash's toArray for the common case of converting/copying a collection.

funcs.name: ToFinite
funcs.signature: func ToFinite(value any) float64
funcs.doc:
ToFinite converts value to a finite float64, mapping NaN to 0 and infinities
to the largest representable magnitudes.

funcs.name: ToInteger
funcs.signature: func ToInteger(value any) int
funcs.doc:
ToInteger converts value to an int by truncating toward zero. Non-numeric and
non-finite inputs yield 0.

funcs.name: ToLength
funcs.signature: func ToLength(value any) int
funcs.doc:
ToLength converts value to a valid array-like length by truncating it to an
integer and clamping the result to the inclusive range [0, 2^53-1]. It
mirrors lodash's toLength.

funcs.name: ToLower
funcs.signature: func ToLower(s string) string
funcs.doc:
ToLower returns s with all Unicode letters mapped to their lower case,
without any word splitting.

funcs.name: ToNumber
funcs.signature: func ToNumber(value any) float64
funcs.doc:
ToNumber converts value to a float64. Booleans become 0 or 1, numeric types
are converted directly, and strings are parsed (with surrounding whitespace
trimmed). Anything that cannot be interpreted as a number yields NaN.

funcs.name: ToPath
funcs.signature: func ToPath(path string) []string
funcs.doc:
ToPath splits a property path string into its component keys. It understands
dotted notation ("a.b.c") and bracket notation with optional quotes
("a[0].b", "a['b.c']"), yielding ["a", "0", "b"] and ["a", "b.c"]
respectively.

funcs.name: ToSafeInteger
funcs.signature: func ToSafeInteger(value any) int
funcs.doc:
ToSafeInteger clamps ToInteger(value) to the range of integers that float64
can represent exactly, i.e. [-(2^53-1), 2^53-1].

funcs.name: ToString
funcs.signature: func ToString(value any) string
funcs.doc:
ToString converts value to its string representation. Nil becomes the empty
string; all other values use fmt.Sprint.

funcs.name: ToUpper
funcs.signature: func ToUpper(s string) string
funcs.doc:
ToUpper returns s with all Unicode letters mapped to their upper case,
without any word splitting.

funcs.name: Transform
funcs.signature: func Transform[K comparable, V, A any](m map[K]V, fn func(acc A, v V, k K) A, init A) A
funcs.doc:
Transform reduces the map into a single accumulated value by applying fn to
each key/value pair, seeded with init.

funcs.name: Trim
funcs.signature: func Trim(s, cutset string) string
funcs.doc:
Trim removes leading and trailing characters found in cutset from s. When
cutset is empty it trims whitespace.

funcs.name: TrimEnd
funcs.signature: func TrimEnd(s, cutset string) string
funcs.doc:
TrimEnd removes trailing characters found in cutset from s. When cutset is
empty it trims trailing whitespace.

funcs.name: TrimStart
funcs.signature: func TrimStart(s, cutset string) string
funcs.doc:
TrimStart removes leading characters found in cutset from s. When cutset is
empty it trims leading whitespace.

funcs.name: Truncate
funcs.signature: func Truncate(s string, length int, omission string) string
funcs.doc:
Truncate shortens a string to at most length runes, appending omission (which
counts toward the length) when truncation occurs.

funcs.name: Unary
funcs.signature: func Unary[T, R any](fn func(...T) R) func(T) R
funcs.doc: Unary caps a variadic function so that it receives at most a single argument.

funcs.name: Unescape
funcs.signature: func Unescape(s string) string
funcs.doc:
Unescape reverses Escape, converting the HTML entities for "&", "<", ">", '"'
and "'" back into their characters.

funcs.name: Union
funcs.signature: func Union[T comparable](slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
Union returns the unique elements from all provided slices, preserving the
order of first appearance.

funcs.name: UnionBy
funcs.signature: func UnionBy[T any, K comparable](fn func(T) K, slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
UnionBy returns the unique elements (by key) from all provided slices,
preserving order of first appearance.

funcs.name: UnionWith
funcs.signature: func UnionWith[T any](eq func(a, b T) bool, slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
UnionWith returns the unique elements from all provided slices using the eq
comparator to detect duplicates, preserving order of first appearance.

funcs.name: Uniq
funcs.signature: func Uniq[T comparable](s []T) []T
funcs.doc:
Uniq returns a new slice with duplicate values removed, preserving the order
of first appearance.

funcs.name: UniqBy
funcs.signature: func UniqBy[T any, K comparable](s []T, fn func(T) K) []T
funcs.doc: UniqBy is like Uniq but uniqueness is determined by the key produced by fn.

funcs.name: UniqWith
funcs.signature: func UniqWith[T any](s []T, eq func(a, b T) bool) []T
funcs.doc:
UniqWith returns a new slice with duplicate elements removed, using eq to
decide whether two elements are equal. The first occurrence of each distinct
element is kept, in order. It is the comparator-based analogue of Uniq for
element types that are not comparable with ==.

funcs.name: UniqueID
funcs.signature: func UniqueID(prefix string) string
funcs.doc:
UniqueID returns a process-unique identifier string composed of the optional
prefix followed by a monotonically increasing counter. It is safe for
concurrent use.

funcs.name: Unset
funcs.signature: func Unset(m map[string]any, path string) (map[string]any, bool)
funcs.doc:
Unset returns a copy of m with the value at path removed, along with a boolean
reporting whether anything was removed. The input map is not mutated.

funcs.name: Unzip
funcs.signature: func Unzip[A, B any](pairs []Pair[A, B]) ([]A, []B)
funcs.doc: Unzip splits a slice of pairs back into two slices.

funcs.name: UnzipWith
funcs.signature: func UnzipWith[A, B, R any](pairs []Pair[A, B], fn func(A, B) R) []R
funcs.doc:
UnzipWith splits a slice of pairs into two slices, then combines them
element-wise using fn.

funcs.name: Update
funcs.signature: func Update(m map[string]any, path string, updater func(old any) any) map[string]any
funcs.doc:
Update returns a copy of m with the value at path replaced by the result of
updater applied to the existing value (or nil when the path is absent).
Intermediate maps are created as needed and the input is not mutated.

funcs.name: UpdateWith
funcs.signature: func UpdateWith(m map[string]any, path string, updater func(old any) any, customizer func(key string) any) map[string]any
funcs.doc:
UpdateWith returns a copy of m with the value at path replaced by the result
of updater applied to the existing value (or nil when the path is absent),
using customizer to create any intermediate containers exactly as SetWith
does. The input map is not mutated. It mirrors lodash's updateWith.

funcs.name: UpperCase
funcs.signature: func UpperCase(s string) string
funcs.doc:
UpperCase converts s to space-separated UPPER CASE words. Like lodash's
_.upperCase it deburrs Latin letters and removes contraction apostrophes
before splitting (see compoundWords), so e.g. UpperCase("À") yields "A".

funcs.name: UpperFirst
funcs.signature: func UpperFirst(s string) string
funcs.doc:
UpperFirst upper-cases the first character of the string, leaving the rest
unchanged.

funcs.name: Values
funcs.signature: func Values[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V) []V
funcs.doc: Values returns the values of the map in unspecified order.

funcs.name: Without
funcs.signature: func Without[T comparable](s []T, values ...T) []T
funcs.doc: Without returns a new slice excluding all the given values.

funcs.name: Words
funcs.signature: func Words(s string) []string
funcs.doc:
Words splits a string into its component words. It understands spaces,
punctuation, snake_case, kebab-case and camelCase / PascalCase boundaries as
well as runs of digits.

funcs.name: Wrap
funcs.signature: func Wrap[T, A, R any](value T, wrapper func(T, A) R) func(A) R
funcs.doc:
Wrap returns a function that calls wrapper with value followed by the caller's
argument, letting wrapper decorate or intercept the bound value.

funcs.name: Xor
funcs.signature: func Xor[T comparable](slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
Xor returns the symmetric difference of the provided slices: the unique values
that appear in exactly one of the slices.

funcs.name: XorBy
funcs.signature: func XorBy[T any, K comparable](fn func(T) K, slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc:
XorBy is like Xor but element identity is determined by the key produced by
fn.

funcs.name: XorWith
funcs.signature: func XorWith[T any](eq func(a, b T) bool, slices ...[]T) []T
funcs.doc: XorWith is like Xor but element identity is determined by the eq comparator.

funcs.name: ZipObject
funcs.signature: func ZipObject[K comparable, V any](keys []K, values []V) map[K]V
funcs.doc:
ZipObject builds a map by pairing each key with the value at the same index.
Extra keys map to the zero value; extra values are ignored.

funcs.name: ZipObjectDeep
funcs.signature: func ZipObjectDeep(paths []string, values []any) map[string]any
funcs.doc:
ZipObjectDeep builds a nested map[string]any by treating each element of
paths as a property path (see ToPath) and assigning the corresponding element
of values at that path. Paths without a matching value receive nil. It
mirrors lodash's zipObjectDeep.

funcs.name: ZipWith
funcs.signature: func ZipWith[A, B, R any](a []A, b []B, fn func(A, B) R) []R
funcs.doc:
ZipWith combines two slices element-wise using fn, producing a slice whose
length equals the shorter input.
